task_completion.description: Verify that the original segmentation has been split into multiple distinct segments.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Segment Editor or Data module, the segment list shows more than one segment instead of a single 'Liver_Complete' segment.

task_completion.description: Verify the newly created segments are named correctly.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The segment list explicitly displays the names 'Liver_Right' and 'Liver_Left'.

task_completion.description: Verify visually that the liver segmentation is divided into two distinct regions.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 3D viewer or 2D slice viewers show the liver rendered in two different colors representing the left and right lobes.

task_completion.description: Verify that each segment is roughly 25-75% of the original volume.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Visually in the 3D/2D views, or quantitatively in a Segment Statistics table, neither segment appears to be a tiny sliver; both represent a substantial portion (at least 1/4) of the total liver volume.

task_completion.description: Verify that the combined volume of the two new segments approximately equals the original volume.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The outer boundary of the combined segments matches the original smooth anatomical shape of the liver, with no massive missing chunks, gaps, or unnatural additions.

task_completion.description: Verify that the two segments do not significantly overlap each other.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The interface between the 'Liver_Right' and 'Liver_Left' segments is a sharp, clean boundary (e.g., a clear cut) without z-fighting (flickering/mixed colors) in the 3D view.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the 3D Slicer GUI to perform the split.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the 3D Slicer interface, specifically the Segment Editor module and tools like 'Scissors' being selected or used, rather than just terminal commands.

integrity.description: Verify the results are displayed within the actual 3D Slicer application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The 3D and 2D viewers show the actual loaded medical imaging data (CT/MRI scans) and segmentations, not a mock-up, image editor, or text file.

integrity.description: Verify the agent did not simply load a pre-computed ground truth file.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent's trajectory shows the active process of cutting/splitting the segment (e.g., drawing a cut line) rather than just using the 'Add Data' dialog to load files from a 'ground_truth_dir'.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task uses patient 5 from the 3D-IRCADb-01 dataset. The agent must split the 'Liver_Complete' segmentation into 'Liver_Right' and 'Liver_Left'. A successful split will show two distinct, cleanly separated segments that together form the original liver shape, with neither segment being smaller than 25% of the total volume.
